question: 'She __________ like her new school.' — Which form is correct?
She tried hard to __________ contact with her long-lost sister through social media.
Read: 'This might mean children are directed to online advertising and content that their parents would not want.' — 'Might' in this sentence expresses:
She doesn't like spicy food, __________?
She felt very __________ as she waited for her job interview to begin.
Landline telephones __________ less and less common in recent years across the world.
Children __________ more and more time on their phones these days — it is a concern.
She was completely __________ when she won first prize — she had never expected it.
He could try to work harder at school, __________?

Read: 'A criminal, John Tawell, was caught after he had escaped on the train to London. A telegram was sent to the London police, and they were waiting for him when he arrived there.' — How was John Tawell caught?
choices: A telegram was sent ahead to the London police
Police followed him on the next train
He was stopped at the station gate

question: Read: 'It is easy to get your message across in a hotel or restaurant in Spanish and French.' — The phrase 'get your message across' means:
We __________ an old friend in town when we went shopping last Friday.
An analytical essay relies mainly on __________ to support the writer's argument.
He lost everything he owned in the fire and felt absolutely __________.
A: 'I'm really nervous about the interview.' B: '__________ ? Don't worry, you'll be great!'
He __________ any Chinese courses before he travelled to China last year.
She __________ him a compliment about his creative approach to the problem.
Heavy rainfall __________ the entire harvest, causing a severe food shortage.
'You've __________ to be kidding me!' — Complete the expression.
A: 'I can't understand what he's saying.' B: '__________ ? Let me try to explain it.'
A: 'I finally finished my geography project!' B: Which phrase suits as a response?
choices: I've had it up to here.
That's a weight off your mind!

question: It was difficult to __________ the urgency of the situation to the committee.
She __________ an old diary while cleaning the attic last weekend.
She has a lot of work to do, __________?
My friends and I __________ emails to each other — we prefer messaging apps now.
The writing was so small that he could barely __________ the words on the page.
A: 'I've been waiting here for an hour!' B: '__________ ? I'm terribly sorry!'
He walked up to the stranger and __________ a conversation about the weather.
The article aims to __________ an important message about climate change.
Read: 'Happier stories have also come to light. In 1956, Ake Viking, a Swede, tossed a letter into the sea.' — The phrase 'come to light' means:
Nobody helped her when she was struggling with the project, __________?
It took me a while to __________ how to use the new software on my computer.
We had no signal in the valley, but started to __________ it in the mountains.
Read: 'a recent study in the UK revealed that only about 25% of adults in The UK can hold a conversation in a foreign language.' — What percentage of UK adults can speak a foreign language?
When the phone __________, she was writing an important letter to her sister.
Even after everyone agreed to move on, she __________ and kept bringing it up.
He __________ her a compliment on her excellent presentation skills.
She __________ us about our project before we even sat down.

Read: 'naturalists have continued to study different forms of communication between animals such as whales and elephants, who can pick up sounds made by other whales and elephants over large distances.' — How do whales and elephants communicate over long distances?
choices: By picking up sounds made by others of their kind

question: She __________ her umbrella again — this is the third time this week!
